nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; typically lives among branching staghorn Acropora corals in lagoons and outer reefs at depths of roughly 2-15 m, hovering among or perching directly on the coral branches. Generally encountered in small aggregations of 5-15 individuals.

diet.notes: Carnivorous. Generally accepts a varied diet of small meaty foods such as frozen mysis shrimp, vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, copepods, cyclops, and finely chopped seafood, and most specimens also take quality marine flakes and small pellets in time. Small mouth size and a tendency to be outcompeted by faster swimmers mean multiple small feedings per day, with food drifting near its coral perch, are commonly advised.

avoidWith: other Gobiodon species in small systems

summary: Tiny bright-yellow coral goby from the Western Pacific that perches among Acropora branches. Generally peaceful, hardy, and well-suited to nano and small reef tanks.
careNotes: The Yellow Clown Goby (Gobiodon okinawae), also known as the Okinawa Goby or Yellow Coral-goby, is a small (commonly reaching about 3.5-4 cm total length) coral-commensal goby from the Western Pacific, ranging from southern Japan south to the Great Barrier Reef and east to Palau and the Marshall Islands. In the wild it lives in close association with branching staghorn Acropora corals, hovering among the branches and perching on them, and is generally encountered in groups of 5-15 individuals. In captivity it is widely considered hardy and beginner-friendly: it accepts a wide range of meaty prepared foods, tolerates a typical reef parameter envelope (around 22-26 C, pH 8.1-8.4, dKH 8-12, SG 1.020-1.025), and is one of the most peaceful fish commonly sold for nano reefs. Retailer sources commonly cite a 10 US gal (~38 L) minimum, but a 75 L (≈20 US gal) or larger system with ample branching rockwork or SPS provides better long-term stability and allows for a pair. Conspecific aggression can be substantial in tight quarters, so multiple specimens should only be attempted in larger tanks (roughly 100 L and up) and ideally introduced simultaneously. Generally considered reef-safe, with two important caveats: it has occasionally been observed nipping at small SPS polyps, and spawning pairs lay eggs on the undersides of Acropora and similar SPS, which can cause localized tissue recession (the coral typically recovers under good conditions). Like most gobies it has been known to jump, so a tightly-sealed lid is generally advised. FishBase notes that Gobiodon okinawae is facultatively air-breathing and has a maximum reported total length of 3.5 cm; IUCN lists the species as Least Concern (2018). Reported lifespans range from roughly 2-5 years in typical care up to 7+ years in well-kept aquariums, with isolated public-aquarium and hobbyist reports of specimens reaching 11-13 years.
breedingNotes: Has been spawned in home aquariums, though raising fry to maturity is generally considered very difficult. The species is a protogynous hermaphrodite of an unusual bidirectional type: any two individuals can form a pair, with the smaller of two males changing to female (or the larger of two females changing to male) as needed. Pairs deposit demersal eggs on the underside of branching SPS corals (typically Acropora), where they are guarded; eggs have been reported to hatch in roughly 5-7 days, after which the planktonic larvae require rotifers and other small live foods. Hobbyist rearing success is rare and survival to adulthood is uncommon.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Followed the gobies collection to the dedicated Yellow Clown Goby product page (https://www.liveaquaria.com/products/clown-goby-yellow). Confirmed: Easy care, peaceful temperament, reef-safe with caution toward SPS polyps, 72-78 F (22-25.5 C), pH 8.1-8.4, dKH 8-12, SG 1.020-1.025, 10 US gal minimum, 1.5 in max, carnivorous diet (mysis, brine, frozen prep foods), Indo-Pacific origin, spawns readily and lays eggs on coral undersides.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed authority (Sawada, Arai & Abe, 1972), Gobiidae / Gobiiformes, Western Pacific distribution (S Japan to S GBR, Palau, Marshall Islands), reef-associated lagoon habitat among Acropora at 2-15 m, max length 3.5 cm TL, wild temperature 20-25 C (preferred 25-29 C), aggregations of 5-15 individuals, facultative air-breathing, IUCN Least Concern (2018).

additional.name: AquaticCommunity (Yellow Clown Goby care guide)
additional.notes: Cross-checked: max ~1.5 in (4 cm), peaceful toward heterospecifics with aggression toward conspecifics in undersized tanks, reef-safe with caveats on SPS, 10 gal minimum for single fish and 30+ gal for multiples, pH 8.1-8.4, SG 1.020-1.025, 72-82 F (22-28 C), bidirectional protogynous hermaphrodite, accepts flake plus varied frozen foods, native to E Indian Ocean and W Pacific.

additional.name: AquaHoy / Aquarium Insiders / Saltwater Aquarium Blog (lifespan and breeding cross-check)
additional.notes: Lifespan estimates collated across hobby sources: typical 2-5 years in average care, 5-7 years with excellent care, exceptional records of 11-13 years (Randall & Delbeek 2009). Confirms spawning readily on Acropora undersides with fry hatching in 5-7 days; hobbyist rearing success rare.

fish.breedingNotes: Pairs spawn readily on the underside of branching SPS corals, but fry are tiny and require rotifers/copepods; survival to adulthood in home aquaria is rare. Any two individuals can form a pair due to bidirectional sex change.
